page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-288-lssd-metal is a bare-metal server instance designed for memory-intensive and high-throughput workloads. It features 288 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6985P-C processors with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The system provides 144 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, supported by a 504 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 2232 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, yielding approximately 7.75 GB of RAM per core.

This server includes 16242 GB of local NVMe SSD storage distributed across six drives, offering substantial high-speed local capacity. In benchmark testing, the hardware demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, scoring 313266.86 in stress-ng multi-core tests and 178741.62 in PassMark CPU evaluations. Memory performance is also highly rated, with uncached large-block read bandwidth reaching 1090.21 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 412.25 GB/sec.

The instance is highly efficient for database operations, achieving 422.06 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and scoring in the top tier for PassMark database operations. It is well-suited for large-scale in-memory databases, software compilation, and cryptographic tasks. However, while prompt processing for medium-sized LLM models is strong, text generation for smaller models falls into the lower performance tiers, indicating a tradeoff when executing specific lightweight inference tasks without dedicated GPU acceleration.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-288-lssd-metal is a bare-metal server configured for memory-intensive and high-compute workloads. It features 288 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6985P-C processors running at 4.2 GHz, 2232 GB of 6 GHz DDR5 RAM, and 16242 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The server delivers top-tier performance in multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations, as evidenced by its stress-ng multi-core score of 313266.86 and Redis throughput of 422.06 million operations per second. It lacks dedicated GPU hardware, which impacts its text generation efficiency for small language models, but it excels in large-scale database management, software compilation, and high-throughput data processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-288-lssd-metal is a bare-metal server with 288 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 2232 GB DDR5 RAM, and 16242 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for high-throughput database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-288-lssd-metal features 288 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 2232 GB DDR5 RAM, and 16.2 TB NVMe SSD storage for database workloads.
tagline: A high-core bare-metal platform combining massive DDR5 memory capacity and high-speed local NVMe storage for demanding database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 288 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6985P-C processors at 4.2 GHz
2232 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
16242 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across six drives
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark results
Optimized for large-scale in-memory databases, Redis, and software compilation
